meaning: v.打嗝,(火山、炮等)冒烟、喷出n.打嗝,喷射
examples: He drank and stifled a belch.
He sat back and gave a loud belch.
I remember, in the play, Sir Toby Belch was a big fat man, who liked drinking too much and having a good time.
They conclude that if all the officials involved are ambitious, America could belch out 13% less by 2020.
One belch from a taco lunch could power NASA's computers for an hour!
